label: Revolutions & Political Change

sources.title: Reform and Revolution in Latin America

app_prompts: Atlantic revolutions comparison: user picks two of the four Atlantic revolutions (American, French, Haitian, Latin American independence) and sees how the causes, ideologies, and outcomes compare.

Revolutionary document close reading: user picks a founding document (Declaration of Independence, Declaration of the Rights of Man, Haitian Declaration) and sees who was included, who was excluded, and why.
Causes of revolution analyzer: user picks a revolution and traces the structural conditions — economic crisis, political exclusion, ideological challenge, state weakness — that made it possible.
Decolonization timeline: user picks a region (Africa, Asia, Caribbean) and traces how colonized peoples won independence — negotiation, armed struggle, Cold War leverage, international pressure.
Revolutionary aftermath quiz: user reads about a revolutionary outcome and traces what happened next — consolidation, betrayal, reaction, or new revolution. What patterns repeat across cases?
